source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
In the following two problems we will look at shooting a basketball. Model the basketball as an elastic hollow sphere with radius 0.1 meters. Model the net and basket as shown below, dimensions marked. Neglect friction between the backboard and basketball, and assume all collisions are perfectly elastic.

<image_1>

For this problem, you launch the basketball from the point that is 2 meters above the ground and 4 meters from the backboard as shown. You attempt to make a shot by hitting the basketball off the backboard as depicted above. What is the minimum initial speed required for the ball to make this shot?
Note: For this problem, you may assume that the size of the ball is negligible.

The shot setup depends on spatial relations among launch point, backboard, basket, and net that are not fully specified in text alone

multi_step: The task asks for a minimum initial speed, which suggests an optimization over feasible trajectories rather than a single direct lookup
Solving requires combining projectile motion with a backboard collision model and basket geometry
question_text includes multiple physical assumptions: elastic collision, neglected friction, launch height, distance from backboard, and diagrammed dimensions
Multiple-choice options are close in value, indicating nontrivial quantitative reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value '7.19'
choices provide a finite set of candidate numeric outcomes for easy answer checking
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true, so core assets are available for downstream verification
